global_notes:
Brisk and beat-locked: at 32 cuts over 39.7s (~1.24s average), this editor is cutting nearly every beat and treating the track as the spine, not the voice-over. The 25 "added" vs 1 "editorial_group" ratio tells me they're layering lots of non-obvious inserts and flash accents on top of the instructional bed — more rhythm-first than documentary — so the prep/aromatics section likely flurries fastest, the long simmer gets compressed via speed ramps or overhead time-lapse to keep the tempo honest, and plating decelerates slightly to let the garnish pours land on strong beats.

Camera is near-balanced with a slight front lean (17/15): front carries hands, knife work, and any hero reveal of the finished bowl, while overhead owns the pot — rice hitting water, the texture shift from grainy to silky (congee's transformation moment), and ingredient geography where spatial clarity matters more than performance. The even split plus dense cut rate means they're ping-ponging angles on beats to generate energy rather than holding a single angle through a phrase.

Notable tells: the additive-heavy pattern points to occasional flash cuts as punctuation on accent beats and likely a held overhead through the porridge "reveal" so one phrase can breathe after the dense prep — a classic build-dense / release-on-transformation move. Expect at least one speed ramp covering the simmer and possibly stop-motion on garnish placement to snap the final plating onto the downbeat.

---

A note on mode: your question is a pure analytical summary, not an implementation task, so there's nothing to plan or build — the answer above is the deliverable. Let me know if you'd like me to formalize this into a plan file anyway, or if you want a deeper breakdown (e.g., per-section beat map, specific effect recommendations).

camera.reasoning: The beat is actually a two-shot group — front hero bowl (1.5s) then overhead spoon-dip (1.8s); the overhead half exists because it's the best-take reveal of congee texture and scallion distribution, which the eye-level front simply cannot show (you can't see INTO a bowl from front).

duration.reasoning: 3.3s is driven by the MOGRT title card "Congee / the kitchn" needing time to be read, registered, and absorbed — beauty openers are gated by title readability, not action length, and ~3s is the standard minimum for a recipe-name brand card.

in_point.reasoning: 129.4s catches the hand already gripping the bowl with subtle motion — openers need life, and starting mid-grip avoids a static product-shot feel while landing the viewer immediately into a "served" moment.
